stem: A building casts a shadow on level ground. The shadow extends 60 feet from the base of the building, and the angle of elevation from the tip of the shadow to the top of the building is 53 degrees. Given that sin 53° ≈ 4/5, cos 53° ≈ 3/5, and tan 53° ≈ 4/3, what is the height of the building, in feet?
explanation: The shadow length (60 feet) is the side adjacent to the 53° angle, and the building height is the side opposite. Using tan 53° = opposite/adjacent: height = 60 × tan 53° = 60 × (4/3) = 240/3 = 80 feet.

distractorRationale.A: Used sin instead of tan, treating the adjacent side as if it were the hypotenuse: 60 × (4/5) = 48.
distractorRationale.B: Divided by tan instead of multiplying, computing 60 × (3/4) instead of 60 × (4/3): 60 × (3/4) = 45.
distractorRationale.D: Solved for the hypotenuse instead of the opposite side, dividing the adjacent by cos 53°: 60 ÷ (3/5) = 60 × (5/3) = 100.

imageDescription: A right triangle showing a vertical building on the left with its shadow extending horizontally to the right. The horizontal shadow is labeled 60 ft. The angle of elevation from the tip of the shadow to the top of the building is labeled 53°. The building height is marked with a question mark.
